title: Pelexia parva
taxonomy: Pelexia parva is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the genus Pelexia and the family Orchidaceae. The genus Pelexia is part of the subfamily Spiranthoideae, which is a subfamily of the orchid family. Pelexia parva is a small terrestrial orchid native to Central and South America. Its classification within the plant kingdom is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Division: Magnoliophyta, Class: Liliopsida, Order: Asparagales, Family: Orchidaceae, Genus: Pelexia, Species: P. parva.

intro: Pelexia parva is a species of orchid native to Central and South America, characterized by its small, delicate flowers and long, slender leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may aid in reducing inflammation and protecting against oxidative stress. Bioactive constituents of Pelexia parva include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pelexia parva include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are often used to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
